# New Zealand vs Egypt Preview: Team News & Prediction | FIFA World Cup 2026 | Group Stage

by The Stats Zone· 19 June 2026 at 1:25 am

Updated 19 June 2026 at 1:26 am

Check out our dedicated FIFA World Cup 2026 hub! Here you can find expert match previews for every fixture at this summer's tournament in USA, Canada & Mexico. All-time records for all 48 nations, key betting stats, player bios, group standings, live scores & plenty more!

After both sides earned a point in their opening game, New Zealand and Egypt now face off in Vancouver with a chance of more-or-less wrapping up knockout stage football.

Kick-off date/time: 22 June 2026 at 2:00 am

How to watch on TV: UK – ITV / USA – FOX Sports

How to watch online: UK – ITVX / USA – FOX One

news_items.title: New Zealand lineup vs. Egypt: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 clash including Elijah Just decision - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
New Zealand lineup vs. Egypt: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 clash including Elijah Just decision - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 2

New Zealand

Vancouver Stadium

Egypt

# New Zealand lineup vs. Egypt: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 clash including Elijah Just decision

By Seye Omidiora| 19 Jun 2026 20:27, Last updated: 19 Jun 2026 20:28

© Iconsport / Nayra Halm/SPP

Elijah Just will hope to repeat his Iran heroics against Egypt as the winger is fully expected to keep his place in the starting XI for Sunday's second Group G fixture.

The wideman registered a superb brace in the opening match to take his international tally to 11 goals, meaning he joins captain Chris Wood and Kosta Barbarouses as one of three players in the current squad to have reached double figures.

Although Wood failed to add to his own 45-goal haul last time out, the veteran forward provided two assists to underline how he can still heavily influence games beyond his clinical finishing.

Manager Darren Bazeley has no fresh fitness concerns to worry about ahead of the clash in Vancouver, though the tournament-ending hamstring injury suffered by Matthew Garbett previously forced a call-up for Logan

Story by

PREVIEW | New Zealand vs Egypt: team news, lineups, predictions (World Cup 22/06)

New Zealand and Egypt go head-to-head at BC Place this Monday in the World Cup. The match will be broadcast live at 02:00 on ITV 1.

In their last fixture, New Zealand shared the spoils 2-2 with Iran (World Cup 2026). In their last outing, Egypt drew 1-1 against Belgium (World Cup 2026).

Advertisement

Advertisement

The last meeting between the two teams ended with Egypt winning 1-0.

news_items.title: New Zealand predicted lineup vs Egypt for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash
news_items.text:
# New Zealand predicted lineup vs Egypt for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash

Published: 2026-06-20T17:20:24+05:30
Source: khelnow.com (khelnow.com)
Language: en

## Story

New Zealand predicted lineup vs Egypt for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash

FIFA World Cup

# New Zealand predicted lineup vs Egypt for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash

By Anubha A Pathak

Follow

Set As Preferred Source

From the court to the press box, Anubha tells sports stories with insight and passion.

Published at :June 20, 2026 at 5:20 PM

Modified at :June 20, 2026 at 5:21 PM

(Courtesy : Getty Images)

## New Zealand will face Egypt on Sunday, June 21.

New Zealand will face Egypt in their final Group G fixture of the FIFA World Cup 2026, with qualification to the knockout stage still within reach. The Kiwis collected a point from their opening match after securing a 2-2 draw against Iran.

With one point from their first game, New Zealand remain firmly in contention ahead of the clash against Egypt.

Coach Darren Bazeley is expected to stick with the same lineup he used against Iran. Some top players are likely to remain in the pivotal roles as New Zealand look for a positive outcome in a game that may be crucial to thei

# New Zealand lineup vs. Egypt: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 clash including Elijah Just decision

By Seye Omidiora| 19 Jun 2026 20:27, Last updated: 19 Jun 2026 20:28

© Iconsport / Nayra Halm/SPP

Elijah Just will hope to repeat his Iran heroics against Egypt as the winger is fully expected to keep his place in the starting XI for Sunday's second Group G fixture.

The wideman registered a superb brace in the opening match to take his international tally to 11 goals, meaning he joins captain Chris Wood and Kosta Barbarouses as one of three players in the current squad to have reached double figures.

Although Wood failed to add to his own 45-goal haul last time out, the veteran forward provided two assists to underline how he can still heavily influence games beyond his clinical finishing.

Manager Darren Bazeley has no fresh fitness concerns to worry about ahead of the clash in Vancouver, though the tournament-ending hamstring injury suffered by Matthew Garbett previously forced a call-up for Logan "}

# New Zealand predicted lineup vs Egypt for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash

By Anubha A Pathak

Follow

Set As Preferred Source

From the court to the press box, Anubha tells sports stories with insight and passion.

Published at :June 20, 2026 at 5:20 PM

Modified at :June 20, 2026 at 5:21 PM

(Courtesy : Getty Images)

## New Zealand will face Egypt on Sunday, June 21.

New Zealand will face Egypt in their final Group G fixture of the FIFA World Cup 2026, with qualification to the knockout stage still within reach. The Kiwis collected a point from their opening match after securing a 2-2 draw against Iran.

With one point from their first game, New Zealand remain firmly in contention ahead of the clash against Egypt.

Coach Darren Bazeley is expected to stick with the same lineup he used against Iran. Some top players are likely to remain in the pivotal roles as New Zealand look for a positive outcome in a game that may be crucial to thei"}
